task_completion.description: Verify the agent has logged into the application as the 'admin' user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Rocket.Chat main interface with the user profile or avatar indicating that the 'admin' user is logged in.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the account preferences or settings menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'My Account' or 'Preferences' modal/page being open in the application.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the specific settings section for message highlights.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Messages' or 'Highlights' tab selected within the account preferences menu.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the exact required keywords into the highlight words input field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact words 'error', 'critical', and 'deploy' entered into the 'Highlight Words' (or similar) input field. They may be comma-separated or appear as distinct tags.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved the highlight configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save changes' button being clicked, a success notification (e.g., 'Preferences saved'), or the settings page showing the keywords persistently in the input field after saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the Rocket.Chat web or desktop UI. There should be no terminal windows executing API requests (like curl) or database commands (like mongo).

integrity.description: Verify the keywords were entered into the actual configuration field.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the keywords are entered into the 'Highlight Words' settings field, not just typed into a chat message box, a general search bar, or a separate text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the screenshots show the actual Rocket.Chat application being navigated, rather than a static image, text file, or mock interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target user account is 'admin'. The exact keywords to be highlighted are 'error', 'critical', and 'deploy'. The application being configured is Rocket.Chat.
